title: Architectural Services

narrative.executive_view: Architectural Services has AI productivity potential in production and administration, but professional accountability, client judgment and field work remain valuable. [S1]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI assists drafting, visualization, specifications, proposals and coordination; responsible control, site judgment and liability remain human.
narrative.value_capture: Project and percentage fees eventually reprice as effort falls, so operators retain only part of savings.
narrative.adoption_timing: Industry evidence shows broad interest but uneven maturity; effective adoption remains below 50% and t50 is positive. [S1]
narrative.consolidation_economics: The supplied target count supports buyability. For valuation, a 2026 ROG transaction study reports a 4.92x median EBITDA multiple for architecture firms in controlling external transactions; this remediation applies that same current benchmark at entry and exit rather than assuming unsupported multiple expansion. [S4]
narrative.terminal_demand: Demand is durable for regulated projects, but residential and alteration exemptions narrow the licensed toll. [S3]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The supplied 5413 role mix is engineering-dominated and is a material proxy mismatch; project-cycle, fee deflation and principal portability remain key risks. The 4.92x architecture transaction median is A/E-study evidence rather than a disclosed $1–10M target series, and its controlling-transaction EBITDA label does not state owner-comp normalization; target diligence must rebuild EBITDA after a market principal salary. [S4]

sources.title: AIA AI research in architecture
sources.what_it_supports: AIA reports only 6% regularly used AI in 2025.

sources.title: Deltek Clarity A&E 2026
sources.what_it_supports: Deltek surveyed 896 A&E firms; AI maturity and ROI remain uneven.

sources.title: NCARB Model Law
sources.what_it_supports: Model law excludes detached one/two-family dwellings and some alterations from licensed practice.

sources.title: ROG 13th Edition A/E Business Valuation and M&A Transaction Study summary
sources.what_it_supports: ROG's 2026 study, derived from 252 transactions and data points, reports a 4.92x median EBITDA multiple for architecture firms in controlling-interest external transactions; it separately labels the minority series pre-owner-bonus.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: AI targets production and administrative tasks rather than accountable design/field work.
caveats: 5413 parent role mix is a proxy.

role_judgments.rationale: Production and administrative tasks automate more than accountable, client, or field work.

buy_mult.rationale: S4 directly reports 4.92x as the median EBITDA multiple for architecture firms in controlling-interest external transactions. This is used as the current entry benchmark without an owner-comp adjustment because S4 does not explicitly state the owner-bonus basis for the controlling series.
caveats: S4 is architecture-specific and transaction-derived but does not disclose a $1–10M EBITDA subsample or an explicit U.S.-only cut.
The controlling-transaction 4.92x line is labeled EBITDA but not explicitly pre-owner-bonus; normalize principal compensation to market before applying it to an owner-operated target.

exit_mult.rationale: No forward exit transaction series was reopened. A defensible base case is no multiple expansion: hold the same current 4.92x architecture controlling-transaction benchmark reported by S4 at exit.
caveats: This is a no-expansion estimate anchored to S4, not an observed future exit price.
S4 is architecture-specific and transaction-derived but does not disclose a $1–10M EBITDA subsample or an explicit U.S.-only cut.
The controlling-transaction 4.92x line is labeled EBITDA but not explicitly pre-owner-bonus; normalize principal compensation to market before applying it to an owner-operated target.

pricing_structure.value: Lump-sum and percentage project fees face effort-based deflation.
pricing_structure.rationale: Conservative selected estimate based on the cited industry evidence.
caveats: Industry and role proxies are imperfect.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Moderate task exposure and positive t50 reflect uneven mature implementation.

terminal_value.justification: Licensing preserves some accountable work but exemptions and AI pressure the production layer.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No; parent 5413 occupation data are engineering contaminated.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Historic-effort fee norms and competitive bids pass through part of production savings.

dataset_mismatch.description: 5413 parent role mix is not code-specific.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The no-expansion base case makes M zero; actual target quality and normalized EBITDA determine whether any exit uplift is achievable.

reviewer_note: Remediates 2026-07-21_gpt-5.6-terra_v312p1_541310: S4 was reopened and directly supports the 4.92x architecture controlling-transaction benchmark. The exit is deliberately held equal to entry because no matched forward exit series supports expansion; owner-comp normalization remains a disclosed diligence requirement.
